mechanism_narrative: A declining FEDFUNDS rate reduces the discount rate applied to future earnings and eases financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next 20 trading days. The signal is confirmed when the policy rate is falling from already low levels and the equity market has not yet repriced the easing.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative or below the unconditional mean in more than 60% of months when FEDFUNDS is declining on a 3-month basis, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above recent levels indicates weakening household income growth and lower cyclical demand, which should pressure equities lower over the next month. The effect is strongest when unemployment jumps after a period of stable labor conditions.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the average return after triggers is not lower than non-trigger periods by at least 1 standard error.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and HOUST both strengthen, it signals broad domestic demand and supportive revenue expectations, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the following quarter. The effect requires synchronized improvement in consumption and housing rather than either series alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition of rising RSAFS and rising HOUST does not outperform the unconditional return distribution over 60-day horizons, or if either proxy alone explains the signal equally well.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in INDPRO signals improving industrial supply capacity and stronger cyclical earnings momentum, which tends to support S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal works when production growth is positive and not merely a rebound from a prior slump.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month changes in INDPRO are followed by negative or flat 20-day SPX returns more often than positive returns, or if the conditional mean return is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in GDPC1 growth after prior expansion implies demand deceleration and weaker profit expectations, which should depress S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The effect is identifiable when quarterly real GDP growth is slowing rather than merely noisy.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 1-quarter annualized growth falls below prior 2-quarter average by > 1pp
failure_condition: Reject if GDP slowdowns defined by the trigger are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of instances, or if the average conditional return is not below baseline by a meaningful margin.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of elevated FEDFUNDS and accelerating CPIAUCSL indicates tightening real financial conditions and inflation pressure, which should reduce S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The interaction matters because high rates are more bearish when inflation is also rising.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-rate/high-inflation state does not produce lower 20-day SPX returns than either high rates alone or inflation alone, or if the conditional hit rate falls below 40% for negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth is strong while UNRATE remains low, labor income and household spending capacity are improving together, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. This signal fails if employment gains occur alongside rising unemployment, indicating poor labor quality.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month change positive and UNRATE not rising
failure_condition: Reject if the combined employment-strength condition is not followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect disappears when controlling for either PAYEMS or UNRATE alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in HOUST signals weakening residential investment and softer future demand for cyclicals, which should weigh on S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days. The mechanism is strongest when housing activity is falling from above-trend levels.
failure_condition: Reject if negative 2-month changes in HOUST are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than chance, or if the average 5-day return after the trigger is non-negative.
